methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Mbodi AI is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $3M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a reference universe of nine private robotics companies (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and one public comparable, though no same-stage or adjacent-stage peers within the robotics sector yielded sufficient data density for traditional peer-median comparison.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach, using the company's $3M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage transitions. This implies a valuation of $7.5M. A sensitivity range around this point, accounting for execution and market timing risk, yields a low bound of $6M and a high bound of $9M.

This analysis is constrained by a sparse peer set at the Seed stage within robotics and manufacturing; while 11 same-sector companies exist in the broader universe, none qualified as directly comparable on stage, funding tier, or valuation proximity. The $6M–$9M range, with a median of $8M, reflects the Stage-Progression method alone. Key uncertainties include founder track record depth, product-market fit validation, and competitive positioning within a fragmented robotics landscape. Updating this valuation will require either narrower peer emergence or material operational milestones.
